16.12.2021 9.11Tissue Sample Processing (Hxxx and Sxxx) · Benchling
Sivu 1/3https://benchling.com/terva_ovca/f/lib_Dsw4n1js-helsinki-dna-damage…issue-sample-processing-hxxx-and-sxxx/view?versionId=32171847#printTissue Sample Processing (Hxxx and Sxxx)IntroductionCheck the sample size and its condition (necrotic, hypoxic, abundant adipose tissue, etc) before determining what could be done with tissue samples.If Ascites are available, only cell culture could be done and FD.If the sample size and the condition is fine, it is used for 3 different purposes: Baseline FFPE experiment, snap-frozen for RNA/DNA extraction (for Barun), establishing cell culture and if there is enough sample then time point IR experiment as well. The first priority is given for Baseline FFPE and snap-frozen. (If the piece is enough only for either cell culture or IR tt then go for IR tt if there is enough piece for both IR and non-IR, if there is not enough for IR/non-IR then establish cell culture only).Materials›10% formalin solution for fixation (prepared in PBS)›scalpel, twizzer, scissor for tissue dissection›warm RPMI 1640 +10%FBS (+glu +pen/strep)›warm OCMI medium for cell culture›pipettes and tips according to the volume used›cassettes›Dispase II (1U/ml i.e.

sor for tissue dissection›warm RPMI 1640 +10%FBS (+glu +pen/strep)›warm OCMI medium for cell culture›pipettes and tips according to the volume used›cassettes›Dispase II (1U/ml i.e. 125 μl/10ml RPMI)›100μm strainer›50ml falcon tubes›cryopreservation vials ›T75 Primaria flasks for cell culture›waterbath set to 37°C›​ProcedureEstablishing cell culture from tissue sample1. Finely dissect the small piece of tissue and transfer into a 50 ml falcon tube containing 10ml RPMI with Dispase II (125 μl/10 ml) and leave for 2 hr digestion at 37°C with occasional shaking.

16.12.2021 9.11Tissue Sample Processing (Hxxx and Sxxx) · Benchling
Sivu 2/3https://benchling.com/terva_ovca/f/lib_Dsw4n1js-helsinki-dna-damage…ssue-sample-processing-hxxx-and-sxxx/view?versionId=32171847#print2. After digestion, solution is filtered through 100 μm strainer.3. Collect the filtrate solution and centrifuge for 5 minutes at 300 rcf.4. Gently remove the supernatant fluid without affecting the cell pellets.5. Dislodge the cell pellets and add 10 ml of previously warmed complete OCMI medium. 6. Gently mix the cell suspension and transfer into T-75 primaria flask.7. Check cells under microscope and tranfer to the incubator.Establishing cell culture from Ascites 8. If the ascites is clear then no need to do RBC lysis beforehand. If the ascites is bloody then treat with RBC lysis buffer (Hybri Max).9. For clear ascites: Centrifuge the falcon tube containing ascites for 5 minutes at 300rcf.10. Gently remove the supernatant and dislodge the cell pellets.11. Add 10ml fresh OCMI and plate on T-75 corning flask. Remember to check the flask under a microscope before transferring it to the incubator.12. If need to freeze down, then instead of OCMI add Stem cell banker (SCB) according to the number of vials (1ml per vial) to be frozen.13.

before transferring it to the incubator.12. If need to freeze down, then instead of OCMI add Stem cell banker (SCB) according to the number of vials (1ml per vial) to be frozen.13. For bloody ascites (if there is solid mass inside the falcon tube then remove that from ascites → gently shake the ascites and then centrifue for 5 minutes at 300rcf.14. Discard the supernatant gently and add the required volume of lysis buffer depending on the cell pellets size (usually if the size of cell pellets in 1 ml cell then add 1 ml of lysis buffer).15. Dislodge it and incubate 10 minute at room temperature.16. Volume makeup to 50ml (if 50ml falcon tube) with either PBS or RPMI (prefer PBS as it is cheaper than RPMI).17. Centrifuge again for 5 minutes at 300rcf. If the ascites is still bloody then repeat the lysis process (max. twice).18. Discard the supernatant.19. Add OCMI and SCB for cell plating and freeze down respectively.

16.12.2021 9.11Tissue Sample Processing (Hxxx and Sxxx) · Benchling
Sivu 3/3https://benchling.com/terva_ovca/f/lib_Dsw4n1js-helsinki-dna-damage…ssue-sample-processing-hxxx-and-sxxx/view?versionId=32171847#printSnap frozen (for Barun)20. Cut the small piece of tissue samples depending on the available size.21. Transfer it into cryopreservation vial and transfer and store at -80°C.Baseline FFPE and IR 22. Label the cassette with a solvent resistant marker.23. A small piece of the dissected tumor is placed in a cassette and transfer to a 10% formalin solution (this will be baseline FFPE).24. For 8 hr IR experiment: Place 1 piece in 6-well plate prefilled with RPMI 1640 +10%FBS (+glu +pen/strep) and incubate (for control). (Note: if the sample is quite small then can use a 12/24-well plate according to the needs).25. Place 1 piece in a falcon tube with RPMI and irradiate at 10Gy (420 seconds) and place in a 6 cm plate prefilled with RPMI 1640 +10%FBS (+glu +pen/strep) and incubate (for experimental).26. After 8 hr post-IR, collect the samples (1 non-irradiated and 1 irradiated) in 2 separate cassettes and transfer into a 10% formalin solution along with the baseline cassettes for formalin fixation at +4°C.27.

he samples (1 non-irradiated and 1 irradiated) in 2 separate cassettes and transfer into a 10% formalin solution along with the baseline cassettes for formalin fixation at +4°C.27. If 24 hr time point experiment is done, the protocol will be the same except there will be 2 non-irradiated and 2 irradiated pieces and the collection will be done at 24 hr exactly the same way as done for 8 hr sample collection.28. After formalin fixation, cassettes are transferred to 70% EtOH and store in a cold room or send for paraffin embedding and sectioning (Note: Room temperature formalin fixation is done not more than 24 hrs whereas in +4°C 3 days is fine).
